title: Solar PV System Engineering & Battery Sizing
subject: Electrical Power Systems
description: Design off-grid solar arrays, calculate peak sun hours, select MPPT charge controllers, size Lithium Iron Phosphate (LiFePO4) battery banks, and wire pure sine wave inverters.
learningObjectives: Calculate daily watt-hour energy consumption and required solar array wattage.
Size MPPT charge controller voltage and current limits under cold-weather Voc expansion.
Select appropriate wire gauge (AWG) to restrict DC voltage drop below 3%.

lessons.title: Energy Audit & Peak Sun Hour Sizing
lessons.content: To size an off-grid system, perform a load audit. Example: 500W well pump running 2 hrs/day = 1,000 Wh. 100W freezer running 12 hrs/day = 1,200 Wh. Total daily consumption = 2,200 Wh. Accounting for 15% system losses (2,200 / 0.85 = 2,588 Wh), dividing by 4.5 peak sun hours yields a minimum array size of 575 Watts.

lessons.title: LiFePO4 Battery Bank Depth of Discharge
lessons.content: Unlike lead-acid batteries (50% max Depth of Discharge), LiFePO4 cells safely tolerate 80-90% DoD over 4,000+ cycles. A 48V 100Ah LiFePO4 bank stores 4,800 Wh of usable energy.

flashcards.definition: Maximum Power Point Tracking: Converts excess panel voltage into higher charging current.
Open-Circuit Voltage: Maximum voltage across panels when disconnected from load; increases in cold weather.
Inverter output waveform identical to utility grid power, required for inductive motors.

questions.question: What happens to a solar panel's Open-Circuit Voltage (Voc) on extremely cold winter days?
options: Voc increases and may exceed charge controller limits

questions.question: If your homestead daily energy demand is 4,800 Watt-hours, how many Amp-hours at 48V DC is required for 1 full day of battery storage?

practicalChallenge.title: Homestead Electrical Load Audit
practicalChallenge.instruction: List all appliances, record their nameplate Wattage, estimate run hours per day, and calculate your homestead's daily Wh demand!
